evals.prompt:
Review this shadcn-style UI snippet and modernize it with brief explanations: ```tsx

Delete project?

This action cannot be undone.

 ```
evals.expected_output: Finds the composition and styling problems, restores accessible dialog structure, and replaces brittle styling with shadcn-friendly patterns.
expectations: Adds `DialogHeader` and `DialogTitle` or equivalent accessible title structure.
Calls out the manual `z-index`, raw colors, and `space-x-*` usage as avoidable.
Prefers variants and semantic tokens over one-off visual overrides.

evals.prompt: Build a settings side panel with shadcn/ui for notification preferences. I need a trigger button, a Sheet, grouped controls, labels, descriptions, and clear guidance on when Sheet is better than Dialog or Popover.
evals.expected_output: Uses `Sheet` intentionally, keeps the structure accessible, and shows grouped settings controls with maintainable composition.
expectations: Explains why a side panel is a better fit than `Dialog` or `Popover` for this case.
Uses `Field`, `FieldGroup`, or similar structured control composition instead of loose spacing wrappers.

evals.prompt: Explain the best-practice Tailwind v4 theming setup for shadcn/ui. I want the CSS variable shape, `@theme inline`, semantic token usage, and what not to do when customizing colors.
evals.expected_output: Explains the modern Tailwind v4 token flow and discourages fragile customization shortcuts.
expectations: Uses `@theme inline` in the explanation or example.
Keeps theme variables in a central CSS entry and maps them to semantic token utilities.
Warns against scattered raw colors or duplicated `dark:` overrides.

evals.prompt: Show a React Hook Form example using shadcn/ui for a bug report form with title, severity, and description fields. I want accessible labels, descriptions, error states, and a maintainable field layout.
evals.expected_output: Uses structured field wrappers and exposes invalid states accessibly.
expectations: Sets `data-invalid` on the field wrapper and `aria-invalid` on invalid controls.
Uses the right component for the severity choice instead of plain buttons.

evals.prompt: I have a dashboard page where every feature folder copied its own `Card`, `Badge`, and `Separator` so the team could tweak spacing and colors per screen. Propose a better shadcn/ui organization and explain what should stay in `components/ui` versus feature-level files.
evals.expected_output: Re-centers the architecture on shared primitives plus feature composition and explains why copy-forking primitives hurts maintainability.
expectations: Keeps primitives in shared `components/ui` or an equivalent shared package.
Places screen-specific composition in feature-level files rather than forking primitives.
Calls out copy-paste customization as a maintainability problem.

evals.prompt: A shadcn project uses both radix-style `asChild` examples and base-style `render` examples in the same code review. Explain how you would review trigger composition safely without assuming the APIs are interchangeable.
evals.expected_output: Shows awareness of the base-vs-radix split and avoids giving a one-size-fits-all fix.
expectations: Explicitly distinguishes base and radix trigger composition APIs.
Warns against assuming one trigger API works for both bases.
Keeps the review centered on correctness rather than generic style advice.
